title: UI Inventory
description: Catalog of the screens and components found in a product's source-code repository. Supplementary artifact of the repo_analysis stage (alongside design_system). Ensures the agent inspects the actual UI code instead of planning around assumptions — the same inspected-not-assumed contract as source_media_review.

screens.description: Screens/pages found in the repo. Each must have been actually read, not guessed from file names.

id.description: Stable slug used by scene_plan scenes to reference this screen (e.g. 'dashboard', 'signup-form')

source_files.description: Repo-relative paths of the files that implement this screen

purpose.description: What the screen does for the user, stated from the code that was read

ui_elements.description: Notable elements on the screen — these become the assembling pieces in the animation

label.description: The element's actual visible text/label from the source, when present

flagship_notes.description: Why this screen is (or isn't) a hero surface for the video

reviewed.description: Must be true — the source was actually read, not assumed

flagship_recommendations.description: Which screens the video should feature, with reasoning — presented to the user at the repo_analysis gate

summary.description: What the product's UI actually consists of — specific, from observation ('a 3-panel dashboard with a usage chart, an invoices table, and a team settings form'), not generic

planning_implications.description: Concrete constraints or opportunities that must affect proposal/script/scene planning. Each item should be actionable.
